feed.title: AO3 - Clark Kent/Bruce Wayne (Filtered)
feed.subtitle: Latest works for Clark Kent/Bruce Wayne on Archive of Our Own [English, No ABO]

entry.title: Free Fall
entry.summary:
Author: Holdt

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 15,708Chapters: 7/9Kudos: 22

Summary: Bruce Wayne has been maintaining a file. It is eleven pages, encrypted, labeled TLI — Tactical Liability Index, and it has absolutely nothing to do with tactics. Clark Kent has been paying attention. These are, it turns out, related problems. (Or: Bruce spends eight months filing his feelings under increasingly implausible headings. Clark waits him out. Alfred makes the Sumatran. Nobody is surprised except Bruce.)

entry.title: The Prince's Pet
entry.summary:
Author: TheBobblehat

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 66,001Chapters: 14/?Kudos: 305

Summary: For the past five hundred years, Krypton has successfully spearheaded the colonization and reeducation of twenty six alien planets. Lorded over by the Royal House of El, Krypton's forces have just declared victory over their 27th conquest. The planet X9Y-BH91, otherwise known as "Earth." As a representative for the planet, and potentially a candidate for the new world order, the Earthling Lex Luthor has come to Krypton with a peace offering; a small selection of humans as gifts. One in particular is promised to the oldest son of the House of El, Kal. But Kal-El's pet is not docile, not subservient, not obedient. And for a prince considered soft to the touch, Kal's pet proves to be one hell of a problem. As for Bruce Wayne, a man who has lost everything--his children, his wealth, his home--no measure is too drastic to escape the planet Krypton. When sheer force does not work, Bruce decides he can seduce his master into a false sense of security before killing him in his sleep and jumping ship back to Earth. A fool proof plan. Or at least, it would have been... had he not fallen in love with Kal-El in the process.

entry.title: Dead Reckoning: Part One
entry.summary:
Author: ChemShaw

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 20,670Chapters: 2/14Kudos: 0

Summary: Five months after disappearing, Aven Davis is running out of time—and out of places to hide. A volatile implant is the only thing keeping the queen inside him dormant, sending him straight into Metropolis and unexpectedly back into Clark Kent’s orbit.Meanwhile, Bruce Wayne’s search for Aven takes a devastating turn, revealing betrayals that put every step he’s taken under Weyland-Yutani’s gaze. With the clock ticking and danger closing in, all three men are pulled toward a collision that will decide whether Aven survives long enough to ever come home. (Please Read Ashes In The Dark part one and Two before Reading.)

entry.title: Being part of the Kent-Wayne family isn’t so bad
entry.summary:
Author: PurpleButterfly19

Rating: Not RatedLanguage: EnglishWords: 9,318Chapters: 3/39Kudos: 46

Summary: It’s been three years since Dick and Kori got married, and many things have changed for the Kent-Wayne family. The family grew larger through more adoptions and also biological ties. New soulmates were discovered, more weddings were held, the Justice League can finally put a name to the man behind the cowl, and Wayne Manor will never look the same again. But the greatest change? Bruce Wayne and Clark Kent are officially engaged. This is the story of how they got there. As for Conner? He couldn’t be doing any better!

entry.title: The Man of Stone of Gotham
entry.summary:
Author: WriterDMathis

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 15,292Chapters: 4/?Kudos: 21

Summary: Sequel to Luminous Fragments From The Veil Above. After the fall of his homeworld, Kal—El begins his new life on Earth, adopted by Bruce Wayne and his four sons. As he discovers Earth, its strange customs, and the warmth of a new family, he also finds love with Bruce—and a home he never thought he’d have. And his Earth name is known as Clark. Clark Wayne.

entry.title: Am I making you feel Sick
entry.summary:
Author: fanofthegirl

Rating: Not RatedLanguage: EnglishWords: 50,209Chapters: 5/?Kudos: 329

Summary: Hal Jordan was dying long before a mission went wrong and his heart finally gave out. He was dying when he signed the divorce papers. Dying without making it anyone else’s problem. No one Noticed.Not Bruce. Not Clark. Not the Justice League. And Hal was fine with that because twenty months ago he signed away the right to have anyone worry about him. He would have kept it that way, too If he hadn’t burned himself out saving the world. If he hadn’t collapsed hard enough that everyone finally had to look. Now Bruce and Clark are hovering like they never spent nearly two years avoiding him. Acting like this is their burden to carry, like they didn’t already decide, once, that letting him go was easier than staying. Hal doesn’t understand it. There’s no cure. If Oa hasn’t found one, there’s no point in pretending otherwise. He’s made peace with dying. Bruce and Clark haven’t. They refuse to give up on him the way they gave up on the marriage. And that more than the poison in his blood or the ache in his chest is what hurts the most. Because Hal is still in love with them. And loving them while they fight to save him might be the one thing he can’t survive.

  • Creator Chose Not To Use Archive Warnings

entry.title: i've always put my cards upon the table/never be said that i'd be unstable
entry.summary:
Author: kay_n

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 1,433Chapters: 1/?Kudos: 8

Summary: While Gotham was characteristically rainy and dark, she had her days. That it was sparse and uncommon was granted; but every once in a while the sun would come out just a little more and the moon would shine just a touch brighter. It was on one of those days-- well, nights-- that Bruce ran into the infamous alien. He had worked with him before on occasional league missions, but never bothered to do more than grunt or give a cursory response when he addressed him. Something about the way he presented himself struck Bruce the wrong way.[...]Bruce may be called the world's greatest detective, but he was actually quite useless when it came to reading people’s intentions when he was involved. He had spent a lot of his childhood among petty friends who took him for granted and wanted nothing more than his money, and that seemed to follow him into adulthood. Or; Bruce can't seem to get a read on this Superman guy-- but that doesn't stop him from wanting to pick him apart to understand what makes him tick. Or take his clothes off. Please enjoy! Feel free to leave a comment or kudos:)Title is from "Just My Imagination" by The Cranberries.

entry.title: Nowhere to Land
entry.summary:
Author: bloodvvitch

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 36,384Chapters: 11/25Kudos: 234

Summary: Bruce – recently injured and working a case that just won’t break – sees the tactical advantages of temporarily ‘dating’ Superman. Clark – who was almost too late to save his friend from a fatal fall – doesn’t understand what he’s signed up for. For two people who know each other so well, the World’s Finest just can’t manage to get on the same page.

entry.title: they're sharing a drink they call loneliness
entry.summary:
Author: spinnychairs

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 2,540Chapters: 1/?Kudos: 9

Summary: It was on a normal patrol when Batman first encountered Clark Kent, dressed up in the rags of drunkenness and looking to interview him. It's this conversation that sends Batman down the spiral that is Clark Kent and Superman, right at the peak of the loneliest time of his life. But even the most jovial of Boy Scouts have their own troubles, and Clark is no exception. He's more than the intruder Bruce thinks of him as, and the Batman finds himself intrigued. Especially when he finds they may share that aching feeling, that craving for others. And it's at a makeshift bar in Gotham, under bulbous lights and the hum of hangovers, where they connect.

entry.title: Places to be
entry.summary:
Author: Sir_Ackermann

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 22,332Chapters: 5/?Kudos: 102

Summary: Bruce has been stuck in a bad mood for weeks, the people around him are tired of his attitude. But it all changes when Alfred and dick convince him to go to a last minute gala. Its there where he meets a certain reporter...   (‼️‼️‼️ON HIATUS DUE TO MENTAL HEALTH PROBLEMS, NOT SURE WHEN IT WILL CONTINUE BUT IT WONT UNFINISHED FOREVER‼️‼️‼️)

entry.title: The cold boy that met the sun
entry.summary:
Author: Arachnophobic_SpiderMan

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 10,145Chapters: 7/?Kudos: 124

Summary: What happens when Alfred Pennyworth finds 15 years old Bruce Wayne's suicide plans ? What happens when Bruce Wayne runs away ? What happens when he finds himself in Smallville, Kansas, on the anniversary of his parent's murder ? He meets Clark Kent, that's what happens.orI need more of fics where Bruce and Clark meet as teenagers. Honestly i wrote this for myself. Tags may be added as the story goes fyi. Also english is very much not my first language and it shows, so no hate pls okayy? thanksss xx

entry.title: ʞuıl ʎɥɔʇǝʞs ɐ ʞɔılɔ noʎ uǝɥʍ suǝddɐɥ ʇɐɥʍ sı sıɥʇ 'ǝǝs
entry.summary:
Author: RenkonNairu

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 3,920Chapters: 1/?Kudos: 36

Summary: Clark Kent is an investigative journalist who's articles have placed three billionaires under legal scrutiny already. Once was lucky, twice was a coincidence, but three times is a pattern and people have already dubbed Kent the 'Billionaire Breaker' for his exposes on the upper class. Brucie Wayne is a billionaire with a secret and he does not want the Billionaire Breaker discovering it. Under the guise of flirting, Brucie texts Clark a link and when the flustered farm boy opens it, the link opens a backdoor hack into his phone. But Batman discovered much more than he expected in the Metropolis reporter's phone. Clark Kent's tech is more advanced than anything else on Earth. Its almost as if Clark is... an alien
